name: Spitting Cave of Portlock
tagline: A dramatic sea cave and cliff viewpoint hidden in a quiet Portlock neighborhood — waves funnel through lava rock and explode upward.
parking: Very limited street parking on Lumahai St — be respectful of residents
hours: Open access (no gates), best during medium-high surf
bestTime: Late afternoon for sunset light on the cliffs; medium-high surf for the best spitting action
talkStory: At the end of Lumahai Street in the upscale Portlock neighborhood, a public access path between two houses leads to one of Oahu's most dramatic natural features. Waves crash against lava rock cliffs and funnel through a sea cave, creating violent spouts that erupt every few minutes. There's no signage, no railing, no infrastructure — just raw coastline. Locals come here for sunset away from any crowds. WARNING: This spot is genuinely dangerous. The cliffs are unguarded and slippery, and at least 8 people have died here. Stay well back from the edge, never turn your back on the ocean, and absolutely do not cliff jump. Respect the neighborhood — park carefully and keep noise down.
